text:
There once was a wise old owl who lived near a forest clearing. Every morning before dawn, he'd settle into his favorite tree branch, overlooking the tranquil glade.

One particular spring morning, the air was filled with a chorus of birdsong. As he surveyed the awakening wildflowers, he had an idea. He flew down from his perch and snuck into the clearing with surprising agility.

Among the rustling leaves, he discovered a small group of kids gathered under a large maple leaf. They were playing tag, chasing each other with abandon. The youngest, a pint-sized adventurer named Willow, happened to be their runner-up.

The owl floated down gracefully and landed lightly beside the kids. Giggles filled the air as Willow ran past him.

"I wish I had wings," Willow shouted as she darted around a particularly daring stunt.

The owl didn't speak, but he knew exactly what Willow wanted. He spread his wings very slowly, letting them flap gently. To Willow's astonishment
